deep_dive.title: One-Shot Novel View and Pose Human Image Synthesis via 3D Prior Guided Diffusion Model
deep_dive.abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of one-shot novel view and pose human image synthesis. The existing methods transfer the reference human image to a target pose using a set of 2D pose keypoints or synthesize human images based on generalizable human NeRF which uses human model priors to extract point-wise features. However, pose transfer based methods can not handle complex human pose using ambiguous 2D pose as the condition, while generalizable human NeRFs may be inaccurate to recover occluded/invisiable human parts without extracted reliable features. To solve these problems, we propose a novel approach for novel view and pose synthesis from a singe human image via conditional denoising diffusion model. Our diffusion model divides the novel view and pose synthesis problem into a sequence of conditional denoising steps. Specifically, to generate humans with complex and arbitrary poses, we introduce 3D human priors, i.e., 3D normal map and color prompt, as geometry and color conditions into the generation process. By transferring the reference human into the target human with a series of diffusion steps, our diffusion model enables high-quality synthesis including the occluded/invisible parts. Further, we propose a self-reconstruction based customized refinement to enhance fine details when tested on novel persons.Experimental results on different public datasets demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms previous methods and also shows better generalization ability across datasets. The code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/Yankeegsj/3DPGDM.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Diffusion model for one-shot human image novel view and pose synthesis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散模型的单张图像人体新视角与姿势合成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于扩散模型的单张图像人体新视角与姿势合成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散模型的单张图像人体新视角与姿势合成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses one-shot novel view and pose synthesis with a conditional diffusion model that leverages 3D human priors to handle occlusions and complex poses, outperforming 2D pose transfer and NeRF-based methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单张人体图像的新视角与新姿势合成难题，现有二维姿势迁移方法难以处理复杂姿势，通用人体 NeRF 在遮挡/不可见部分恢复不准确。本文提出一种基于 3D 先验引导的条件扩散模型，将任务分解并融入人体模型先验，有效处理复杂姿势与遮挡。实验表明所提方法在生成质量和姿势准确性上优于以往技术。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对单张人体图像的新视角与新姿势合成难题，现有二维姿势迁移方法难以处理复杂姿势，通用人体 NeRF 在遮挡/不可见部分恢复不准确。本文提出一种基于 3D 先验引导的条件扩散模型，将任务分解并融入人体模型先验，有效处理复杂姿势与遮挡。实验表明所提方法在生成质量和姿势准确性上优于以往技术。

deep_dive.title: PermaVid: Consistent Video Generation Across Edits via Disentangled Context Memory
deep_dive.abstract: Consistent video generation under editing operations requires persistence: when edits modify scene appearance or layout, subsequent generations should remain coherent across time and viewpoints. However, existing memory designs struggle to maintain long-term consistency after such modifications, as stored contexts may become outdated or invalid. To address this, we propose PermaVid, a novel framework built upon a multi-modal context memory that disentangles spatial context into semantic appearance and geometric structure, together with an edit-aware memory update and retrieval strategy that keeps memory evolution aligned with subsequent observations. Specifically, we develop two complementary memory banks: an RGB context memory that captures appearance-aware observations while implicitly encoding geometry, and a depth context memory that preserves geometry-only structure disentangled from semantics. Building on this design, we introduce a memory-guided video generation model that performs multi-modal feature fusion under reference conditions drawn from mixed-modality memory contexts. Experiments demonstrate that our method maintains strong long-term semantic and structural consistency after edits, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: consistent video generation under editing via disentangled context memory
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过解耦上下文记忆实现编辑下的一致性视频生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过解耦上下文记忆实现编辑下的一致性视频生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过解耦上下文记忆实现编辑下的一致性视频生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PermaVid maintains long-term consistency in video generation across edits by disentangling spatial context into appearance and geometry, with edit-aware memory update and retrieval.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为应对视频编辑后维持长期时空一致性的挑战，提出PermaVid框架，将空间上下文解耦为语义外观和几何结构，分别存储于RGB和几何记忆库，并设计编辑感知的记忆更新与检索策略；在多种编辑操作下能保持连贯稳定的视频内容生成，解决了传统记忆在编辑后失效的问题，为可编辑视频的连贯生成提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为应对视频编辑后维持长期时空一致性的挑战，提出PermaVid框架，将空间上下文解耦为语义外观和几何结构，分别存储于RGB和几何记忆库，并设计编辑感知的记忆更新与检索策略；在多种编辑操作下能保持连贯稳定的视频内容生成，解决了传统记忆在编辑后失效的问题，为可编辑视频的连贯生成提供了新方案。

deep_dive.title: PermaVid: Consistent Video Generation Across Edits via Disentangled Context Memory
deep_dive.abstract: Consistent video generation under editing operations requires persistence: when edits modify scene appearance or layout, subsequent generations should remain coherent across time and viewpoints. However, existing memory designs struggle to maintain long-term consistency after such modifications, as stored contexts may become outdated or invalid. To address this, we propose PermaVid, a novel framework built upon a multi-modal context memory that disentangles spatial context into semantic appearance and geometric structure, together with an edit-aware memory update and retrieval strategy that keeps memory evolution aligned with subsequent observations. Specifically, we develop two complementary memory banks: an RGB context memory that captures appearance-aware observations while implicitly encoding geometry, and a depth context memory that preserves geometry-only structure disentangled from semantics. Building on this design, we introduce a memory-guided video generation model that performs multi-modal feature fusion under reference conditions drawn from mixed-modality memory contexts. Experiments demonstrate that our method maintains strong long-term semantic and structural consistency after edits, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: enables consistent video generation under editing operations via disentangled memory
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过解耦记忆实现编辑操作下的一致视频生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过解耦记忆实现编辑操作下的一致视频生成

deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频编辑后长期一致性问题，提出解耦空间上下文为语义外观与几何结构的两项记忆库，并设计编辑感知的记忆更新与检索策略，确保后续生成与观察对齐，实现跨时间与视图的连贯视频编辑。

quick_skim.title: Mean-Field Parallel Decoding for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Discrete diffusion language models enable parallel token generation, offering a pathway to low-latency decoding. However, selecting tokens independently by marginal confidence limits effective parallelism: tokens that appear reliable in isolation can form incompatible configurations when several positions are updated at once. We introduce a training-free decoding framework that coordinates these parallel updates. At each forward pass, the method assigns a commit score to each masked position and refines these scores using pairwise interactions derived from the model's predictive distributions. A variational relaxation yields a simple fixed-point update that suppresses conflicting simultaneous commitments within a single forward pass. This mechanism allows the decoder to commit more tokens in parallel while maintaining competitive generation quality. The method is lightweight, requires no auxiliary model or retraining, and drops into existing diffusion decoding pipelines without modification. Experiments on reasoning and code-generation benchmarks show consistent improvements in the quality-latency trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes training-free parallel decoding for discrete diffusion language models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出针对离散扩散语言模型的无训练并行解码方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出针对离散扩散语言模型的无训练并行解码方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出针对离散扩散语言模型的无训练并行解码方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Discrete diffusion models enable parallel decoding but suffer from incompatible token predictions; this paper introduces a training-free mean-field decoding framework coordinating updates via pairwise interactions for faster and more consistent generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 离散扩散语言模型面临并行解码时标记预测不一致的问题。本文提出无训练的平均场解码框架，通过成对交互协调并行更新，在单次前向传播中抑制冲突，实现更快更一致的生成。该方法有效提升了扩散模型的并行解码效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 离散扩散语言模型面临并行解码时标记预测不一致的问题。本文提出无训练的平均场解码框架，通过成对交互协调并行更新，在单次前向传播中抑制冲突，实现更快更一致的生成。该方法有效提升了扩散模型的并行解码效率。

quick_skim.title: ControlMap: Controllable High-Definition Map Generation for Traffic Scenario Simulation
quick_skim.abstract: Simulation is central to validating autonomous driving systems, yet current pipelines are limited by insufficient scenario diversity due to costly High Definition (HD) map creation. Scaling HD maps requires expensive data collection and manual processing. Moreover, existing generative models lack the fine-grained control necessary to target specific road topologies during generation.   This paper presents a data-driven pipeline for controllable HD map generation using latent diffusion and ControlNet for spatial conditioning. To our knowledge, we are the first to inject spatial guidance signals into a diffusion model for HD map synthesis. Furthermore, our model supports adjustable conditioning strength through classifier-free guidance and city-level style transfer via city label conditioning. To complement existing metrics, we introduce two novel metrics to evaluate adherence to the control signal and similarity to ground-truth maps. Experiments demonstrate that our model generates realistic HD maps that faithfully follow input road topologies while accurately preserving city-specific details.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: latent diffusion and ControlNet for spatial conditioning in HD map generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用潜在扩散和ControlNet进行空间条件高清地图生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用潜在扩散和ControlNet进行空间条件高清地图生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用潜在扩散和ControlNet进行空间条件高清地图生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper presents a data-driven pipeline using latent diffusion and ControlNet for controllable HD map generation, enabling spatial conditioning and adjustable strength for traffic simulation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自动驾驶仿真中高精地图构建成本高、生成缺乏控制的问题，提出基于潜在扩散和ControlNet的可控高清地图生成流水线，通过空间引导信号和可调节条件强度实现道路拓扑的精细控制。实验表明该方法能生成多样化且可控的地图，首次将空间条件引入扩散地图合成，有助于增强仿真场景多样性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对自动驾驶仿真中高精地图构建成本高、生成缺乏控制的问题，提出基于潜在扩散和ControlNet的可控高清地图生成流水线，通过空间引导信号和可调节条件强度实现道路拓扑的精细控制。实验表明该方法能生成多样化且可控的地图，首次将空间条件引入扩散地图合成，有助于增强仿真场景多样性。

quick_skim.title: Through the PRISM: Preference Representation in Intermediate States of Video Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Evaluating video generation with clean, pixel-based reward models disconnects evaluation from the noisy diffusion process and incurs massive VAE decoding costs. In this paper, we challenge this paradigm by asking a fundamental question: Can a powerful video generator inherently discriminate preferences directly from noisy latents? To answer this, we introduce \textbf{PRISM} (\textbf{P}reference \textbf{R}epresentation in \textbf{I}ntermediate \textbf{S}tates of Diffusion \textbf{M}odels). PRISM employs a lightweight Query-based Aggregation head with a frozen video diffusion backbone to decode preference signals from noisy latents. Surprisingly, PRISM not only achieves SOTA preference accuracy but also unlocks strong noise-robustness, which enables early-stage Best-of-$N$ sampling. This allows for filtering suboptimal candidates at the very beginning of denoising, drastically reducing computation while boosting video quality. We also reveal a strong positive correlation between a backbone's generative performance and its inherent evaluative power, enabling self-improving video backbones.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: preference evaluation from noisy diffusion latents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从噪声扩散潜变量进行偏好评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从噪声扩散潜变量进行偏好评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从噪声扩散潜变量进行偏好评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PRISM evaluates video diffusion models directly from noisy latents using a lightweight head, achieving SOTA preference accuracy without costly VAE decoding, and exhibits strong noise robustness.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视频生成评价需将潜变量解码为像素再评估，计算开销大且与扩散过程脱节；PRISM利用冻结的视频扩散模型骨干和轻量查询聚合头，直接从中间噪声状态解码偏好信号，在多个基准上达到最先进准确率，并展现了对不同噪声水平的鲁棒性，显著降低了评价计算成本，为扩散模型的在线高效评估开辟了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有视频生成评价需将潜变量解码为像素再评估，计算开销大且与扩散过程脱节；PRISM利用冻结的视频扩散模型骨干和轻量查询聚合头，直接从中间噪声状态解码偏好信号，在多个基准上达到最先进准确率，并展现了对不同噪声水平的鲁棒性，显著降低了评价计算成本，为扩散模型的在线高效评估开辟了新范式。
